description: CVE ID parameter interpolated into URL without format validation. f-string URL construction (line 122: url = f"{self._base_url}?cve={cve_id}") does not validate that cve_id matches CVE-YYYY-NNNNN format. Malformed input could inject additional query parameters via & characters.
fix: Add CVE ID format validation (regex r'^CVE-\d{4}-\d{4,}$') at the port/domain boundary before the value reaches any adapter.

description: Unbounded in-memory cache (self._cache: dict[str, EPSSScore]) with no size limit or TTL. Processing a large number of unique CVE IDs could lead to memory exhaustion (application-level DoS).
fix: Add a max cache size (e.g., LRU with maxsize) or use a TTL-based cache. Consider functools.lru_cache or cachetools.TTLCache.

description: CVE ID parameter interpolated into URL without format validation. f-string URL construction (line 137: url = f"{self._base_url}?cveId={cve_id}") does not validate CVE ID format before use.
fix: Add CVE ID format validation (regex r'^CVE-\d{4}-\d{4,}$') at the port/domain boundary before the value reaches any adapter.

description: Unbounded in-memory cache (self._cache: dict[str, NVDEnrichment]) with no size limit or TTL.
fix: Add a max cache size or TTL-based eviction policy.

description: Unbounded in-memory cache (self._cache: dict[str, GitHubAdvisory]) with no size limit or TTL.
fix: Add a max cache size or TTL-based eviction policy.

description: API key included in POST request body (payload['api_key'] = self._api_key) rather than in an Authorization header. While this follows Tavily's documented API, request bodies are more likely to be logged by proxies/WAFs than auth headers.
fix: No code change needed — this is Tavily's required API pattern. Ensure HTTP access logs do not capture POST bodies.
